# Formal Specification

## Entities

### MindId

Globally unique identifier for a mind.

### Identity `Ι`

Immutable description of a mind:

```text
Identity := { id, parent_id?, kind, created_at, version }
```

Identity is created once and never mutated.

### Lawbook `Λ`

Set of rules that validate the next state before commit.

Current rule types:

```text
RequireKey(key)
ForbidKey(key)
ImmutableKey(key)
```

### State `Σ`

Mutable symbolic cells:

```text
State := Map<String, SymbolValue>
SymbolValue := Null | Bool | Number | Text | List<SymbolValue> | Map<String, SymbolValue>
```

State changes only through `StatePatch`.

### Gateway `Γ`

Projection layer. It exposes state summaries and accepted commits without granting mutation authority.

### History `H`

Append-only causal commit chain:

```text
Commit := {
  id,
  proposal_id,
  mind_id,
  parent_commit?,
  actor,
  reason,
  at,
  patch,
  before_hash,
  after_hash,
  judgment
}
```

## Proposal validity

A proposal is valid when:

```text
proposal.mind_id == target_mind.id
patch != ∅
∀ op ∈ patch: op does not violate invariants
Λ(next_state) == pass
```

## Evolution function

```text
Evolve(Mind, Proposal) -> Result<(Mind′, Commit), Error>
```

Operational trace:

```text
M0 = current mind
P  = proposal
S0 = M0.state
S1 = apply(P.patch, S0)

valid_identity(P, M0)
valid_invariants(P.patch, S0)
valid_lawbook(S1, M0.lawbook)

C = commit(P, hash(S0), hash(S1), judgment)
M1 = M0 with state=S1 and history=H+[C]
```

## Fracture conditions

Reject without mutation if any condition occurs:

- wrong target mind
- empty patch
- immutable key changed
- required key removed
- required key missing after patch
- forbidden key present after patch
- child attached to the wrong parent
- child count exceeds invariant limit

## Replay specification

Given identity `Ι` and event stream `H`, replay must deterministically reconstruct state `Σ`:

```text
replay(Ι, H) → Σ
```

Replay is valid only if:

```text
record sequence is contiguous
record previous hashes form a chain
record hashes match record bodies
commit parent links match stream order
commit before_hash equals current reconstructed state hash
commit after_hash equals next reconstructed state hash
lawbook validation passes at each step
invariants accept each patch
topology effects are valid for the reconstructed parent
```

## Projection specification

A projection is valid only if it is generated by an explicit policy:

```text
project(mind, policy) → MindProjection
```

The default API projection scope is `public`, not `internal`.


## Nested topology specification

Child attachment is valid only when:

```text
child.parent_id = parent.id
child.id ∉ parent.children
parent.children.len < parent.invariants.max_children
parent state receives child registry cells
commit topology contains AttachChild(identity)
```


## v4 governance extension

Lawbook evolution is represented by a signed causal commit, not by an out-of-band config edit.

```text
Λᵢ --LawbookMigration(operations)--> Λᵢ₊₁
```

A migration is accepted only when:

```text
from_version == current_lawbook.version
to_version == current_lawbook.version + 1
operations != ∅
protected foundation rules are not removed by default
next_lawbook validates next Σ
commit.lawbook_transition.before_hash == hash(Λᵢ)
commit.lawbook_transition.after_hash == hash(Λᵢ₊₁)
commit is appended before live mutation
signature covers lawbook_transition when signing is enabled
```

Snapshots are valid only when:

```text
hash(snapshot.state) == snapshot.state_hash
hash(snapshot.lawbook) == snapshot.lawbook_hash
hash(snapshot body) == snapshot.snapshot_hash
snapshot.after_record_hash connects to the first tail event
```

Replay audit is valid only when the event chain, commit chain, state hashes, lawbook transition hashes, signatures, and snapshot hash checks all succeed.


## v5 specification fragment

```text
Schema migration is accepted iff version = current + 1 and checksum is stable.
Snapshot compaction must never remove the newest keep_latest snapshots.
Observability events are external to Σ and cannot authorize or mutate state.
```


## v6 specification fragment

```text
Request is accepted only if declared body size ≤ max_body_bytes and rate bucket has capacity.
Telemetry export must not mutate Σ.
Backup is valid iff backup_hash, manifest counts, event chain, optional signatures, snapshots, and mind-id constraints all validate.
Restore must write to new files unless an explicit replacement mode is selected by maintenance code.
```

## v7 specification fragment

Identity assertion:

```text
ExternalIdentityAssertion := ⟨source, subject, issuer?, audiences, roles, attributes, client_certificate_sha256?⟩
IdentityBindingPolicy ⊢ assertion ⇢ VerifiedIdentity
VerifiedIdentity ⇢ Principal
```

Signing boundary:

```text
Commit.signable_payload → payload_hash
LocalEd25519(payload) → CommitSignature
ExternalSigningRequest(payload_hash, payload_hex) → external signature completion
```

Object backup pointer:

```text
ObjectBackupPointer := ⟨object_id, backup_id, location, backup_hash, content_hash, size_bytes, created_at⟩
verify(pointer) ⇔ load(location).backup_hash = pointer.backup_hash ∧ MindBackup.verify()
```

Distributed append authority:

```text
append_allowed(plan) ⇔ plan.validate() ∧ plan.allow_local_appends = true
mutation_request rejected before evaluate/sign/append when append_allowed(plan) is false
```

## v8 specification extension

```text
OIDCJWT(token, jwks, policy) → VerifiedIdentity
ManagedSign(commit, key) → request; Complete(request, signature) → CommitSignature
CloudBackupPlan(backup, target) → provider upload plan
Replicate(cursor, records, term) → batch; Verify(batch, cursor) → ack
```

Additional invariants:

```text
JWT accepted ⇔ signature verifies ∧ issuer allowed ∧ audience required ∧ kid exists
Managed completion accepted ⇔ request fields match ∧ payload hash matches ∧ commit signature verifies
Cloud plan emitted ⇔ backup verifies before plan generation
Follower ack accepted ⇔ sequence continuity ∧ hash-chain continuity ∧ signature policy satisfied
```

## v9 specification extension

```text
ReplicatedEventStore.append_replicated_records(records)
```

Preconditions:

```text
records.len ≥ 0
∀r ∈ records : r.mind_id = records[0].mind_id
records[0].sequence = local_last_sequence + 1
records[0].previous_record_hash = local_last_record_hash
∀r : r.record_hash = hash(r.sequence, r.mind_id, r.commit_id, r.previous_record_hash, r.commit)
if signatures_required then ∀r : verify(r.commit.signature)
```

Postcondition:

```text
H_follower' = H_follower ++ records
```

No record is recomputed during follower ingestion.


## v11 operational evidence specification

A scheduled job is valid when target is non-empty, max_attempts > 0, and payload_hash is derived from the serialized payload. A provider receipt is valid when it targets the same execution id/adapter/command/target and a successful receipt observes the expected payload hash. A consensus commit certificate is valid when all votes target the same entry hash and accepted votes meet membership quorum.

## v12 operational specifications

### Scheduler lease invariant

```text
lease valid ⇔
  lease.job_id = job.job_id
  ∧ lease.job_payload_hash = job.payload_hash
  ∧ hash(claim) = lease.claim_hash
  ∧ now < lease.lease_expires_at
  ∧ lease.status = active
```

### Provider SDK receipt invariant

```text
receipt valid ⇔
  receipt.invocation_id = invocation.invocation_id
  ∧ receipt.sdk = invocation.sdk
  ∧ receipt.command_kind = invocation.command_kind
  ∧ receipt.target = invocation.target
  ∧ receipt.observed_request_hash = invocation.request_hash
```

### Consensus apply invariant

```text
apply allowed ⇔
  certificate.verify(active_membership)
  ∧ certificate.committed
  ∧ certificate.entry.operation_kind = replication_batch_commit
  ∧ batch.verify(follower_cursor)
```
